Phew, phew... Calm down, Abraham. As a member of the Curio Collection Squad, you must always stay calm.
恐惧若被旁人察觉，不仅会失去信任，借机为阿尔弗瑞德报仇的愿望也会落空。
Fear, if picked up by others, will not only make everyone distrust you, but also ruin your chance to avenge Alfred.
我还记得你，怕死的伙计。
I remember you. You are that guy who is morbidly scared of death.
记得我？我、我也记得你…但我现在真的一点也不怕、怕死……
Remember me? I—I also remember you... But now I'm not a—afraid of death anymore...
与我朝夕相处的朋友已经率先步入了永夜，我的生命亦不知将在何时终结。
The friends who had once stood by my side day and night have already taken the lead in entering the eternal night, and yet I'm still here, not knowing when my life will end.
Life is but a path to death, but not today.
在为阿尔弗瑞德报仇之前，我还不想死在那些怪物手中！
I don't want to die at the hands of those monsters until I avenge Alfred!
辛克尔夫人把我从障碍判别终端调到了这里，让我跟着奇物再收容小队的温明德队长，在前线磨砺意志。
Mrs. Hinkel transferred me here from the Obstacle Detection Terminal so that I could follow the lead of Wen Mingde, head of the Curio Collection Squad, to toughen myself up on the front line.
但我一过来就碰到了件难事，就是、就是……
But as soon as I came here, I encountered a problem about, about...
重新激活那边的界域定锚！
Reactivating the Space Anchor over there!
你也这么觉得吧？！太好了，我不是一个人……
You think so too!? Great, I'm not alone.
可别这么说…你是拯救空间站的大英雄，这不是人人都能做到的事，谦虚不得……
Don't put it like that... You are the big hero who saved the space station. This's not something that anyone can do. You are being too modest...
The solution is just a few steps away!
别看这几步路，要我鼓起毕生的勇气，也走不到……
It may look like a few steps, but I can't do it, even if I muster all my courage...
其实，激活本身不是难事，只需要走近界域定锚，你我的生物电场就会让它重新运转……
Well, actually, the activation itself isn't the difficult part. You just need to get close to the Space Anchor, and the biological polarity of your body will be enough to activate it.
阻挠我的，是盘踞于必经之路上的怪物。
The real challenge is all the monsters along the way.
以前在障碍判别终端，它们不过是远方的虚影，我的眼里只有蔑视。
Back when I was working at the Obstacle Detection Terminal, they were nothing but distant shadows that I could just brush aside.
直到这次入侵，我得知阿尔弗瑞德死于它们手中时，我的心中开始充斥着憎恨……
Then the invasion happened. And when I learned that they got Alfred, my heart began to fill up with hatred.
而当我成为奇物再收容小队一员，与它们面对面的时候，我却感到了——恐惧！
But when I became a member of the Curio Collection Squad and met them face to face, all I felt was fear!
那一瞬间，我的脑海中闪过阿尔弗瑞德那双逐渐涣散、黯淡的瞳孔。紧接着我呼吸急促，一身冷汗，双腿也打起颤来……
At that moment, Alfred's fading eyes flashed in my head. I felt short of breath, with cold sweat all over my body and my legs trembling...
很窝囊吧？无论如何在心里打气，我就是做不到激活界域定锚，还拖延了好几天…
What a loser, right? I just couldn't find the courage to go through with it and activate the Space Anchor. And it had been like this for several days...
So, {NICKNAME}, do you think you can...
我真的很钦佩你。你坚实的底气，向死而生的勇气，多少也感染了我。
I really admire you. Your perserverance and courage in the face of death have inspired me.
我也很清楚自己几斤几两，我会在这里等你的消息。
I'm well aware of my limitations, so I'll just wait here for an update from you.
Let's go together. We can look out for each other.
不不不，我不行的，一起去反而会帮倒忙……
N—N—No, I can't. I'll just get in the way...
{NICKNAME}，<color=#dbc291ff>激活界域定锚</color>这事就拜托你了！
{NICKNAME}, please <color=#dbc291ff>activate the Space Anchor</color>. I am counting on you!
左「巡猎」，右「同谐」，「智识」在心间……
The Hunt on the left, Harmony on the right, and Erudition in the heart...
好像有声音…但无论发生什么情况，即使在下一秒死去，我也得把口诀念完。
I can hear something... but no matter what happens, even if I die the next second, I must finish reciting this phrase.
左「巡猎」，右「同谐」，「智识」在心间，「存护」在胸前！
The Hunt on the left, Harmony on the right, Erudition in the heart, and Preservation on the chest!
咦，你这么快就回来啦？没想到这条鼓劲口诀我才念了<unbreak>167</unbreak>次，界域定锚就被激活了。
Wow, you are back already? I only recited this morale chant <unbreak>167</unbreak> times, and the Space Anchor is already activated.
咦，是你回来啦。这么快？这条鼓劲口诀，我才念了<unbreak>167</unbreak>次而已……
Hey, you're back already! So soon! I've only recited this morale chant <unbreak>167</unbreak> times.
在你开口前，请允许我将它完整念完，否则，我会非常难受。
Before you say anything, please allow me to finish reciting it, otherwise, I will feel very uncomfortable.
Yes, I am going as fast as I can.
左「巡猎」，右「同谐」，「智识」在心间，「存护」在胸前！
The Hunt on the left, Harmony on the right, Erudition in the heart, and Preservation on the chest!
呼…好了。做得很好，亚伯拉罕。
现在，让我们回到正题吧，我看到那边的界域定锚已被重新激活。
Now, let's get back to business. I see that the Space Anchor over there has been reactivated.
这是我编纂的星神护心诀，可不能随便改词，以免冲撞了星神。
This is the chant of Aeon protection that I came up with myself. Please don't alter the wording, lest we offend the Aeons.
不过我没想到这条口诀才念了<unbreak>167</unbreak>次，你就激活了界域定锚。
But I only recited this chant <unbreak>167</unbreak> times and you already activated the Space Anchor.
其实刚刚念着鼓劲口诀，我也已经想明白了…既然{NICKNAME}都帮我完成了任务，让我还能留在小队中，我就一定要找出杀死阿尔弗瑞德的怪物，为他报仇。

Actually, when I was reciting the chant, I figured it all out... Since you've helped me complete my task already, I can now continue to stay on the team. And I will eventually hunt down the monster that killed Alfred and avenge him.
我会证明给你看，在死亡的长夜降临之前，我一定会勇敢地迈出那一步。
I will prove to you that I have the courage to take that step before the long night of death.
希望这不是你我的最后一面，有缘再会，{NICKNAME}……
I hope this is not our last encounter. Farewell, {NICKNAME}...
How come you never have anything good for me?
啊？这难道不能算好事一桩吗……
Huh? Isn't this a good thing?
有了它，除开个别隐秘之地，<color=#dbc291ff>你就可以顺畅进出各舱室</color>。
With this, <color=#dbc291ff>you can travel between the various zones with ease</color>, except for certain hidden places.
——什么「智力竞赛为荣，体力消耗为耻」，都是杜撰！体力工作，难道就不是工作了吗？
...What is this? "Battle of intellects is a source of pride. Physical exertion brings shame"? It's complete nonsense! So physical labor doesn't count as work now?
今天，我就要杀杀这坏风气。
Today, I'm gonna show them all what real work is about!
在场所有科员都给我听好了——我们空间站「黑塔」，正处于艰难的复兴时期。
All staff present, attention please — Herta Space Station is undergoing a difficult period of revival.
与「军团」一役，朝夕相处的科员惨遭怪物屠戮，他们冰冷不瞑的身首被抛入寂静的寰宇……
In the battle with the Legion, some of our colleagues were slaughtered by monsters, and their cold bodies were thrown into the silent space.
数据入侵抹消了多年的研究心血；受损的奇物在无重力环境下缓慢崩解。
The cyber attack has erased years of research efforts. The damaged Curios are slowly disintegrating in a weightless environment.
目力所及之处都化为了「毁灭」的焦土，怪物余孽仍在银光闪烁的舱室内巡回。
Everything within sight has been turned into a wasteland, and monsters are still roaming around in our cabins.
无论你曾孤独地取得过多少耀眼的成绩，过往那种闭门造车的生活已经行不通了。
No matter how many dazzling achievements you have made on your own, we can no longer afford to live like hermits.
我们需要动员每一位科员的力量，放下固有的偏见，长期通力地合作，回收流散的奇物，修缮破损的舱室。
We need to mobilize every member of our staff, put down our inherent prejudices, and work together to recover the lost Curios and repair the damaged cabins.
安静！如果有科员心怀不满，请你当面向我反映。
Silence! If any staff member feels unhappy with this, please air your grievances to me face to face.
如果各位没有异议，我不希望再听到任何对体力劳动的抱怨，也不想再看到任何工作推诿。
If you don't mind, I don't want to hear any more complaints about physical labor, nor do I want to see anyone putting off work.
有志于奇物回收工作的科员，请尽快到基座舱段的温明德队长手下的奇物再收容小队报道。
If you are interested in Curio recovery, please report to the Curio Collection Squad under Mr. Wen Mingde in the base zone as soon as possible.
有志于修缮工作的科员，现在就到我这里熟悉流程。
Those who are interested in repair work, come to me now to learn the process.
I can help with the repairs.
好，很好。你是曾在主控舱段帮忙的年轻人？我有一项特殊任务要拜托你。
Good, good. Are you the young {M#man}{F#lady} who helped out in the master control zone? I have a special task for you.
每位科员早晚都要担起责任。空间站如今百废待兴，我相信，每个人都会有他的用武之处。
Every staff member must take responsibility sooner or later. The space station is now in dire need of restoration. I believe we can use everyone's help.
我记得你是曾在主控舱段帮忙的年轻人？那更好了，我有一项特殊任务要拜托你。
I remember you. You are the young {M#man}{F#lady} who helped out in the master control zone. Great. I have a special task for you.
距离你我现在所处位置，出门<unbreak>45</unbreak>米，有一处重要的道路缺损。
<unbreak>45</unbreak> meters out the door from where we are now, there is a major road defect.
我查探过，有怪物占领了缺损彼端的小型防卫库，普通科员恐怕无法解决。
I've checked, and the small ordnance depot on the other side of the defect is swarming with monsters. I'm afraid ordinary staff won't be able to deal with this.
Don't worry. Just leave it to me.
Looks like I am the only one...
嗯，<color=#dbc291ff>务必修复道路缺损，清除扼守终点的怪物。</color>
Yes, <color=#dbc291ff>make sure to repair the road and eliminate the monsters at the end of it.</color>
我手上还有诸多事宜，请你完成任务后，向我汇报。
I have many other matters to attend to. Please report back to me when you finish the task.
年轻人，你回来了，嘘，不必多说。让我闭上眼，「检阅」你的工作。
{F#Young lady}{M#Young man}, you're back. Let me concentrate and inspect your work.
声源到达两耳的时间差在<unbreak>0.5</unbreak>毫秒左右，说明声源在三点或九点钟方向。
There is a <unbreak>0.5</unbreak> ms time delay between the sound reaching both ears, which indicates that the source is in the 3 or 9 o'clock position.
左耳声级大于右耳，确定在九点钟方向。说明三点钟方向的怪物已被清除。做得好。
The sound is much louder in the left ear, which confirms the 9 o'clock position. That means the monsters in the 3 o'clock position have been eliminated. Good job.
简单的听声辩位而已，不值一提。
It's just sonar positioning. Nothing to brag about.
在我们「黑塔•星域界种科」，每一个人在跟银河生物打交道的过程中，都会磨练出这样的本领。
In the Herta Department of Ecology, we deal with all sorts of cosmic creatures every day. Developing this kind of ability is just part of our training.
「黑塔」科员本领各异，多接触一些，你就见怪不怪了。
The staff of Herta Space Station possess many different abilities. After meeting a few of us, you won't be surprised anymore.
你没受过训练，听不见也正常。这是空间站内界种科员独有的听声辩位技术。
You're untrained. It's normal that you can't hear anything. This is a sonar positioning technique that is unique to the space station's Ecology staff.
啊，也就是「黑塔•星域界种科」，其中每一个人在跟银河生物打交道的过程中，都会磨练出这样的本领。
In the Herta Department of Ecology, we deal with all sorts of cosmic creatures every day. Developing this kind of ability is just part of our training.
「黑塔」科员本领各异，多接触一些，你就见怪不怪了。
The staff of Herta Space Station possess many different abilities. After meeting a few of us, you won't be surprised anymore.
Why don't you just take a look...
我也应该反思自己，是否也和众人一样，陷入了对「智识」的路径依赖，忽略了简单有效的身体力行。
I should also reflect on whether I, like so many others, have fallen into the trap of overreliance on Erudition and disregarding the much simpler and perhaps more effective methods.
总之，多谢你为「黑塔」做出的贡献。这是我向艾丝妲站长申请的奖励，请收下。
In short, thank you for your contribution to Herta Space Station. This is the reward I managed to secure for you from Lead Researcher Asta. Please take it.
Thank you. You seem to care a lot about this place.
No need to reward me for such a minor good deed.
不，正是一分一毫的小事，才支撑起了复兴的全局。
I disagree. All these minor acts of kindness are precisely what make the reconstruction possible.
当我还是一个籍籍无名的小科员时，第一批为建设「黑塔」耗尽心力的人曾告诉我，「这个宇宙并不友好，它是广阔而黑暗的。」
Back when I was an unknown researcher, I was told by the first group of people who worked on the construction of Herta Space Station, "This universe is never friendly. Rather, it is vast and dark."
在这幽茫宇宙中，有无数双眼睛在盯着「黑塔」，其目的各有不同。
In this vast universe, there are countless pairs of eyes staring at the space station, with various intentions.
有人觊觎它的奇物，有人嫉妒它的中立与自由。
Some covet its Curios, while others envy its neutrality and freedom.
也有人轻视它，把它当作可以随意破坏和践踏的棋子。
Others despise it or see it as a chess piece that can be destroyed and trampled on at will.
或许只有我们这群科员明白，它曾带给我们的喜悦、忧愁、顿悟与漫长的陪伴……
Perhaps we researchers are the only ones who understand the joy, sadness, epiphany, and companionship that it has brought us.
那么也将靠我们，为了保卫它而付出一切。
Which is why it is up to us to give everything we have to protect it.
——谁？甚么人在此骚扰？若是<color=#dbc291ff>水魈</color>冒替，还不速速现出原形！
...Who? Who is interrupting me? If it is the <color=#dbc291ff>Xenohydro</color>, then show yourself at once!
咳，原来是{NICKNAME}，你也是来报名奇物收容志愿者…等等，你莫要过来！
Ahem, so it's you, {NICKNAME}. You're also here to volunteer in the Curio Collection Squad... Wait, don't come over here!
Just say one or two words.
我不同你开玩笑，你必须说个一二字。
I'm not kidding. You have to say one and two.
倒也不必笑得如此夸张。
You don't have to laugh like that.
倒也不必笑得如此夸张。
You don't have to laugh like that.
…发音无异常水声，瞳孔反射正常，泌汗反应正常。
... No abnormal watery sound in your pronunciation. Pupillary reflex normal, perspiration reaction normal.
好，现在你可过来一点，额头让我摸一下——
Okay, now you can come over and let me touch your forehead—
那<color=#dbc291ff>「异星水魈」</color>可做不到如此生动…哎，这千头万绪可又断喽。奇物收容作为「复兴宣言」的重中之重，实践起来却是难上加难。

The <color=#dbc291ff>Xenohydro</color> can't achieve this level of likeness... Alas, the lead was once again inconclusive. Even if the recollection of Curios is the top priority in the Declaration of Revival, it is proving to be a difficult task in practice.
你也知道，自「黑塔」建站以来，围绕奇物收容就设立了<color=#dbc291ff>界种、法政、密卷、地概、应物</color>五个科室。

As you know, since the establishment of the Herta Space Station, five departments have been established to focus on the collection and study of Curios, including <color=#dbc291ff>Ecology, Geopolitics, Mysticism, Geography, and Implement Arts</color>.
每个科室都珍藏着数以百计的藏品。由于此次怪物入侵，约莫有大半都在冲击中流散。
This monster invasion caused the loss of roughly half of the hundreds of collections held by each department.
它们形貌各异，形状不一。有的大，有的小，有的静置无害，有的却无端流毒，更别提其中还有一些能跑能跳的生物型藏品，令人殊为头痛……

They come in all kinds of sizes and shapes. Some are inactive and harmless, some emit poison for no reason, not to mention some biological types of Curios that can plan their own escape, which is a real headache.
有一种名为<color=#dbc291ff>「异星水魈」</color>的奇物，可以任意幻化成他人相貌，顶着各重假身份，到处为非作歹。

There is a Curio called the <color=#dbc291ff>Xenohydro</color> that has the ability to replicate the appearance of other people at will. This allows it to wreak havoc and confusion wherever it goes, as it can easily assume various false identities.
对，就是那个我组建的奇物再收容小队嘛，是空间站依据灾后复兴需要，临时牵头成立的应急小组。
The Curio Collection Squad is an emergency team I put together. The team's mission is to support post-disaster reconstruction and ensure that all of its needs are met during this difficult time.
顾名思义，主要负责回收因怪物入侵而收容失效的奇物。
As the name suggests, it is mainly responsible for collecting the Curios that have been displaced due to the recent monster invasion.
辛克尔夫人那段著名的「复兴宣言」，你想必也早已有所耳闻吧。
Surely, you must have heard of Mrs. Hinkel's famous Declaration of Revival, right?
「朝夕相处的科员惨遭怪物屠戮，他们冰冷不瞑的…」
"Our fellow researchers were slaughtered by monsters. Their lifeless forms were cold and woeful..."
哎，想不起来了。毕竟辛克尔夫人那派头，不是我温某人动辄就可以学来的。
Alas, I can't recall the exact wording. After all, Mrs. Hinkel's style is unique and not easily imitable.
Well, you truly are a man of dedication, Mr. Wen.
是啊，都怪那水魈诡计多端，最近把我捉弄得苦不堪言！这不，才有刚刚你我那番古怪的对话。
*sigh* It's all because of that Xenohydro's trickery. This has been tormenting me to no end! That's why we had to have that conversation earlier.
小队缉拿水魈也有一段时间了，却无功而返。我手底下还有一名叫做<color=#dbc291ff>亚伯拉罕</color>的成员失踪了…可谓屋漏偏逢连夜雨。

The squad has been searching for the Xenohydro for some time, but to no avail. A member of the squad named <color=#dbc291ff>Abraham</color> has even gone missing... It is true what they say — when it rains, it pours.
Missing? I can help with this.
那太好了，我正愁抽不出人手前去找人。
That's great. I was just getting worried that I can't spare anyone to go find him.
这位失踪的亚伯拉罕很容易辨认，他戴着眼镜，身形瘦弱，还总是念叨着「人总是要死的」。
Abraham is easy to identify. He is thin, wearing glasses, and always mumbling about "Life is but a path to death."
就算忽略他的容貌，你只要耐心旁听他的自言自语，不出五句，必有这一句。
You don't even pay attention to how he looks. You can just take a moment to listen to the words being said. He somehow always manages to drop this line at least once every five sentences.
Is it the Abraham I know?
哦？你认识他吗？他是最近才从辛克尔夫人手下调入小队的一名成员，总是念叨着……
Oh, you know him? He was recently transferred to this squad from Mrs. Hinkel's, always mumbling about...
Life is but a path to death.
没错！这口头禅在他的自言自语中，不出五句，必有一句。
That's right! He says this at least once out of every five sentences.
据小队队员向我汇报，亚伯拉罕最后一次出现是在收容舱段。
According to the squad members, Abraham was last seen in the storage zone.
哎，虽然亚伯拉罕常年散发着负能量，他的低气压，大家都苦之久矣……
*sigh* Even though Abraham can be quite the downer, which has been a real torture for his colleagues...
Still we can't just leave him.
但我们也不能扔下他不管。
But we can't just leave him.
逃逸奇物目前都有队员负责，<color=#dbc291ff>「异星水魈」</color>交给我来烦恼，寻找<color=#dbc291ff>亚伯拉罕</color>只能靠你了。

Right now, all squad members are busy searching for missing Curios. And I'll also have to handle the whole <color=#dbc291ff>Xenohydro</color> situation. So it's all up to you to find <color=#dbc291ff>Abraham</color>.
如果能将他安全找回来，我会备下应物科的薄礼，答谢你救了我的燃眉之急。
If you bring him back safely, the Department of Implement Arts will reward you quite handsomely.
今天，今天我就要为阿尔弗雷德讨回公道！
T—Today I'm going to get justice for Alfred!
Whoa, whoa — don't come near me, go away—
去他个大扳手，我信了艾丝妲那丫头说空间站会扩招的鬼话！
Darn it! I can't believe I actually bought that Asta girl's nonsense about how the station is on a hiring spree!
没人处理阻塞，连老头子我也「阻塞」在这里。
No one's even watching the blockade, and now my old butt is blocked here too.
当代的年轻人唷，一天到晚只晓得折腾我这把老骨头……
Darn the kids these days. They only know how to rattle my old bones...
怎么了？没瞧见我给卡这儿了吗？当代的年轻人，脑子跟不上，眼睛怎么也不好使啊。
What's the matter? Don't you see I'm stuck here? Young people these days. Your brain's fried, and your eyes ain't no good either.
哎哟喂，真不愧是当代的年轻人，年纪轻轻一身病，耳朵不好使那就用眼睛看啊，我卡了！
Oh my gosh, the kids these days. Have you lost your hearing? At such a young age? Then take a look with your eyes. I'm stuck!
要我说，这事儿本来也不应该有，处理轴承阻塞一直是两人一组，一人守上面，一人下去，就担心斥力桥出故障，但谁能想到现在年轻人没人愿意干这活。

If you ask me, I'd say this shouldn't have happened. There should always be two people dealing with bearing blockages, one up top and one at the bottom, in case of repulsion bridge failures. But who could've guessed that none of the kids these days are willing to do this job now?
{F#丫头}{M#小子}，斥力桥的配控装置就在我对面，你坐电梯下来重启下，让我过来，就没你事了。
{F#Lass}{M#Lad}, the control device of the repulsion bridge is right opposite me. Take the elevator and reset it to let me come over, and then you can get out of here.
赶紧的，报酬少不了你的。
Hurry up. I won't let you go empty-handed.
How much will you pay me?
啧，你们年轻人就知道搞钱，少不了你的。
Gee, kids these days, all you know is money.
那一定是钱还不够，发挥你的想象力，我满足你。
So it's not enough then. Fine, use your imagination, I will pay you whatever you want.
好{F#丫头}{M#小子}！你去动动那边的配控装置，就能到我这边来…咳、咳咳。
Good {F#lass}{M#boy}! If you operate on the control device over there, you can come to me... Cough, cough.
我在这儿困了大半天，缺水，缺食物，缺氧，没力气说话了，得保存体力。
I've been trapped here all day. I'm hungry, thirsty, and running out of oxygen. I don't have the strength to speak. I have to reserve energy.
{F#丫头}{M#小子}你动作还挺快，这边好东西不少，你自己找找啊。我有急事，先走了。
So, you've got some pretty quick moves. There are lots of nice things here. Help yourself. I got other things to attend to. Bye.
快快快，让开让开，我憋不住了。有事到机修间找我！
Come on, get out of the way, I can't hold it anymore. Come get me in the maintenance room if you need anything!
一个人管着这么多资料啊。
You're responsible for all these archives?
♪ One, two, three, one, two, three, drink... ♪
这些录像带呵，得想办法把它们找回来。
不会错的。我虽然老了，星神的隐秘也忘得差不多了。但这些录像带的数量，我都当身家性命一样记着——年轻人来得正好，如果有空，帮我把东西寻回来吧。

I know I am right about this. I may be getting old. I may have forgotten all about the Aeons... but I keep track of the number of these tapes like my life depends on it. Kid, you came just in time. If you have time, go find them for me.
如果没空，那我就只能找那些老东西来帮我了。
If you don't, then I suppose I'll have to get help from those old farts.
没错。机修间天天跟零件聊天的温天翁，地概科会给花草卷轴起昵称的伯纳德，法政科睁着眼睛说瞎话绝不脸红的古恩……

Yeah. There's Wen Tianweng, who talks to machine parts all day in the maintenance room. Bernard in the Department of Cosmic Geography who gives nicknames to the scrolls and plants. And there's Gunn, who can lie through his teeth without a blink of an eye.
让合适的老东西去办合适的事——适才适用，嗯，适才适用。
Yep, there's an old fart suited to every job.
空间站里的录像带与一般俗物不同，记录了别处都看不到的<color=#dbc291ff>奇景秘闻</color>。正因如此，才需要专人管理。

The videotapes in the space station are different from ordinary ones. They are recordings of <color=#dbc291ff>wonders and mysteries</color> you won't find anywhere. Because of this, they need to be looked after by specialized personnel.
此次我授权你前去回收，正是增长见识的好机会，别人求也求不来。
This is a great learning opportunity for you. You should count yourself lucky.
还是别麻烦老东…老年人了。
Let's not trouble those old fa... elderly people.
好呀好呀，像你这么古道热肠的年轻人，已经不多了。
Good, good. We don't see many accommodating young fellows like you nowadays.
不过，我得提醒你，这些录像带的制作者，为了防范他人窥探隐秘，在录像带中植入了防卫机制。
However, I have to remind you that the producers of these videotapes have implanted some defense mechanisms to help protect their secrets.
如果在接触录像带时不够小心，轻则感到眩晕，强制中断信息摄入；重则会致人行为异常，混淆现实与印象的边界，导致记忆与意识的紊乱。

If you are not careful when touching the videotapes, best-case scenario is you will feel a little dizzy, and the data transfer may be forcibly interrupted. Worst-case scenario, it may cause abnormal behaviors, hallucination, and paranoia, eventually leading to memory and consciousness disorders.
还好我对秘密不感兴趣。
Good thing I'm not interested in secrets.
在空间站这个奇异之地，有时候，就算你阖上双目，闭塞双耳，那些与你缘定的信息依然会钻入你的脑中。
In this bizarre space station, sometimes even if you cover your eyes and ears, the information destined for you will still find a way into your brain.
因为秘密也会挑选它的见证者——这一向是密卷科所虔信的理念。
Secrets choose their witnesses — this is a fundamental belief of the Department of Insight.
与其说是陷阱，不如说是科员们切磋技艺的日常，通过技术把无能之辈挡在信息的大门外。

Not so much of a trap. It's just a result of the constant arms race between researchers vying to be the best at what they do. They simply used a certain kind of technology to turn away those deemed unworthy from accessing information.
利用快闪的视讯号，几不可闻的超声音波，在信息的深处植入危险的诱饵，操弄潜意识实施对其主体的反噬……
Malicious baits are deeply embedded in the information by means of flashing visual stimuli and inaudible ultrasound waves, thus manipulating the subconscious to inflict harm on the subject.
意识、前意识、潜意识；本我、自我、超我；想象、象征、实在…如此种种，关于人心的迷宫结构，是几乎所有黑塔科员都精通的必修课。

Consciousness, preconsciousness, and subconsciousness; self, ego, and superego; imagination, symbolism, reality, and so on. The labyrinth of the human mind is a compulsory subject that almost all space station researchers are proficient in.
Is it too late for me to refuse now?
别狡辩了，老婆子见过许多人，你不像是会拒绝秘密的那一类。
Stop being coy with me. I have met a lot of people in my life. You don't seem to be the kind who would refuse secrets.
如果我告诉你，别去想「大象」——此时你的脑海一定全是那庞大的灰色身体。
If I tell you not to think about an elephant — then images of the large gray animal must be all over your mind.
而我已经告诉你了有关录像带的一切——在你真正得知其中的秘闻之前，你不会轻易将危险遗忘。
And now I've told you everything about the videotapes — you won't easily forget about the danger until you find out the secrets.
据我推测，是有人趁着「军团」入侵，在主控舱段无人值守时拿走了这些录像带。
I am guessing someone took advantage of the Legion's invasion and snatched the videotapes when the master control zone was unguarded.
没有借阅记录，说明嫌疑人不愿暴露身份；没有破坏痕迹，说明他并非为了销毁录像带，而是为了观看其中的秘密。

There are no borrowing records, indicating that the suspect is unwilling to expose their identity. There was no sign of damage, indicating that they were not trying to destroy those videotapes but to view the secret within.
而播放录像带的装置，就在<color=#dbc291ff>支援舱段的安全区</color>。你到那里去，或许就能找到线索。
The device for playing those videotapes is located in the <color=#dbc291ff>safe area of the supply zone</color>. If you go there, you may find some clues.
你紧盯着熄灭的屏幕，若隐若现的荧光似乎要进行危险的倾诉。感应到来者的靠近，画面中快速闪动着金与紫的线条。
You stare at the empty screen. The faint fluorescent light seems ready to begin its dangerous display. Sensing your approach, lines of gold and purple flash rapidly on the screen.
仿佛突然堕入无始无终的空间，镜头终于聚焦——你正透过一副最常见的航行面罩观看着庞然的宇宙腹地。在邈远的深处，点缀着一颗尖锐的黯淡蓝点，仿佛凝结的水滴。你认出来，那是，「黑塔」空间站。

The point of view appears to be falling into empty space without beginning or end. The camera eventually gets focused — now you're looking at the vast hinterland of the universe through a standard-issue navigation visor. In the distance is a bright blue dot, like a condensed water droplet. You recognize it as the Herta Space Station.
下一秒，结晶的裂纹在屏幕上无声蔓延开来。黯淡的蓝色闪烁，怪物残影漫天疾行，沉默巨大的星球开始缓慢裂解。接下来，不断闪回的是襁褓里的啼哭，青年时期的欢笑与愤懑，和临行前「黑塔」冰冷的倒影。

The next second, crystallized cracks spread silently across the screen. The dim blue flickers, monsters spread all over the sky, and the silent planet begins to crack slowly apart. Then comes the flash back, from a baby crying in the cradle, to the joyous laughter and the resentment in teenage years, and the space station's cold reflection before the final departure.
听说人在入灭之前将会回顾一生的记忆，这卷录像带毫无疑问记录着一个已罹难之人的终末时刻。你奋力将自己从身临其境的黑暗泥淖中拔出，只听见一段断断续续的宇宙无线电……

They say your life flashes before your eyes at the final moment of your life. This video undoubtedly records the last moments of a deceased person's life. You struggle to pull yourself out of the immersive dark mud. All you hear is the intermittent cosmic radio...
它似乎在叫着一个你无法听清的名字。
It seems to be calling a name you cannot hear clearly.
总觉得这趟回收之行有危险…
它仍然在叫着一个你无法听清的名字。
It is still calling the name you cannot hear clearly.
简直就像观看记忆一样神奇。
It's amazing, like watching someone's memory.
它仍然在叫着一个你无法听清的名字。
It is still calling the name you cannot hear clearly.
你紧盯着熄灭的屏幕，若隐若现的荧光似乎要进行危险的倾诉。感应到来者的靠近，周遭莫名响起了邈远的窸窣之声，令人不寒而栗。

You stare at the empty screen. The faint fluorescent light seems ready to begin its dangerous display. Sensing your approach, there was an indescribable rustle that sounded off from the surrounding distance, enough to run a chill down your spine.
镜头对准议事间雪白的桌面，开始缓慢平摇，场面致密且嘈杂。一个威严的声音正在宣读布告，关于「黑塔•银河法政科」的建立。

The camera centers on the white table in a conference room and begins to shake slowly. The scene is dense and noisy. Then a majestic voice starts reading a report about the establishment of the Herta Department of Galactic Geopolitics.
威严之声说道，当星神的意志被奉为圭臬，相应地，人的意志便从历史中退场。
The voice says that when the will of the Aeons is regarded as the standard, the will of man will withdraw from history accordingly.
问题就在于，在我们的世界中，有利于星神之举，可以从命途中斩获力量，而强者才能制定规则，力量才能定义正义。
The problem is that our world favors the Aeons, who can reap power from their Paths. Here, the strong get to make the rules and power defines justice.
法政科要反对的正是这一套强者正义论的框架。那些受星神漠视的权益，将由我们敬告；那些遭派系践踏之人，将由我们托起。

What the Department of Galactic Geopolitics wants to oppose is this status quo of the strong ruling over the weak. Those rights and interests ignored by the Aeons will be upheld by us. Those who are trampled will receive our help.
我们致力于在银河中重建属于人的正义价值，这就是两位创始人——古恩•科诺列夫和希拉•诺娃的提议。
We are committed to rebuilding the value of justice for humans in the cosmos. This is the proposal of the two founders, Gunn Konolev and Sheila Nova.
话音未落，议事间内响起了掌声和起哄声，该称他们为法政科的科诺列夫夫妇！在这群情激昂的时段，你看到无数双手从镜头面前交握而过，直到你听见有人在唤一个名字……

Before the voice finishes, there is already applause and jeers in the Conference Room. So they are the Konolevs of the Department of Galactic Geopolitics! During this passionate event, you see countless handshakes in front of the camera until you hear someone call out a name.
希拉！在这铿锵有力的唤声响起的同时，一些背景音正与它密不可分地交缠——这就是星神力量最直接的证明人所希求的正义在一颗星球注定的亡兆面前不值一提殊为可笑——你一边奋力分辨这个名叫希拉的名字，一边被那幻影般的连珠话语所淹没。

"Sheila!" A powerful voice calls out, accompanied by background noises that are hard to separate from it, as if inextricably intertwined. This is the most direct proof of Aeonic powers: In the face of the destruction of the planet, the justice sought by men is laughably insignificant. While you struggle to distinguish the name Sheila, you are drowned out by the murmurs in the background.
录像带的末段模糊不清。
The end of the video seems to be blurry.
突然，你头痛欲裂，不得已放弃了思考。
Suddenly, you get a splitting headache, forcing you to give up the thought.
这两位法政科员听着很耳熟。
Those two geopolitics researchers sound familiar.
突然，你头痛欲裂，不得已放弃了思考。
Suddenly, you get a splitting headache, forcing you to give up the thought.
你紧盯着熄灭的屏幕，若隐若现的荧光似乎要进行危险的倾诉。你静静等待了一阵，但是什么也没有发生。
You stare at the empty screen. The faint fluorescent light seems ready to begin its dangerous display. You wait quietly for a while, but nothing happens.
屏幕亮起，支援舱段已经大开，但宇宙航器的启停仍然受到管控。中微子爆发的隐约微光染黄了月台的夹角。在令人不安的寂静中，你听见了越来越急促的呼吸……

The screen lights up. The supply zone is wide open, but the spacecraft is still under control. The faint glimmers from neutrino explosions stain the platform a shade of yellow. In the disturbing silence, you hear increasingly rapid breathing...
呼吸。奔跑。打开舱门，被锁定的导航界面，换下一个。直到你被一双手拦了下来，它们属于…一位正值壮年的法政科员，他有着鹰的眼神。

Breathe. Run. Open the hatch, lock the navigation interface and move on to the next one. Until you are stopped by a pair of hands. They belong to... a young geopolitics researcher with eyes like an eagle's.
「天鹰堡」已经被毁灭，他说，这就是星神力量最直接的证明。人所追求的正义在一颗星球注定的亡兆面前，不值一提，殊为可笑！
Eagle Castle has been destroyed, he says. This is the most direct proof of the power of the Aeons: In the face of the destruction of the planet, the justice sought by men is laughably insignificant.
他压低了声音，如今宇宙航路已被那个东西阻塞…你不可能赶在「反物质军团」降临前，成功赶回母星示警。
He lowers his voice. "Now that the cosmic route is blocked by that thing... There's no chance you can rush back to your home planet to sound the alarm before the Antimatter Legion arrives."
唯一的可能，是你在路上就被只有毁灭欲望的孽物所截杀。答应我，不要回去送死。在这里，继续我们的研究。
If you do that, you will be intercepted along the way by some evil monster with nothing but the desire to destroy. Promise me, don't go back to die. Stay here, continue our research.
就在下一秒，科员被推到一旁，身后露出了一架完好的航器。在升空尾流的噪音中，你听见那名法政科员大喊着一个名字，仿佛是……
The next second, the researcher is pushed aside and an intact aircraft can be seen behind him. Amidst the noise of the lift-off, you hear the geopolitics researcher shout out a name, as though...
仿佛曾在一段无线电里听过。对不起，嘈杂的无线电中传出疲惫的声音，「黑塔」在「军团」入侵的事件中选择中立。不会有援军抵达，如果可以，请按照预定的航线尽快返回，你听见了吗？

As though you have heard it before on the radio. "Sorry," a tired voice comes through from the noisy radio. "The space station chose to remain neutral in the invasion by the Legion. No reinforcements will arrive. If you can, please return as soon as possible according to the scheduled route. Do you hear me?"
看起来军团不是第一次干坏事。
It seems this is not the first time the Legion played the bad guys.
断断续续的宇宙无线电重复地叫着一个名字。没有回答。
Intermittently, a name is repeated on the cosmic radio. No answer.
某位科员曾离开「黑塔」报信…
A researcher once left the space station with information.
断断续续的宇宙无线电重复地叫着一个名字。没有回答。
Intermittently, a name is repeated on the cosmic radio. No answer.
这几卷录像带，在内容上似有交叉…应该记录了同一个人的不同人生阶段。
These videotapes seem to be related... they seem to be records of different stages of life of the same person.
一个<color=#eb4d3dff>宇宙无线电</color>叫做「希拉」<color=#eb4d3dff>希求的正义</color>的<color=#eb4d3dff>一颗星球注定的亡兆</color>法政科员。
A <color=#eb4d3dff>space radio</color> called Sheila <color=#eb4d3dff>pursued justice</color> for <color=#eb4d3dff>a doomed planet</color> geopolitics researcher.
脑海中的语言突然变得断裂颠倒，昏暗的舱室逐渐在你眼中扭曲成碎异之景。
The sentences in your mind suddenly become broken and disordered. In your vision, the dark cabin gradually becomes distorted and strange.
你头痛欲裂，下一秒便失去了意识。
You get a splitting headache and then lose consciousness.
The medical cabin of Herta Space Station.
你因为不当的信息接触操作晕倒了。可以告诉我，你都接触了什么信息吗？
You fainted because of contact with improper information. Can you tell me what information you have been exposed to?
The life of a geopolitics researcher.
一个对抗「军团」之人的生平…
The life of someone who fought against the Legion...
(Also the life of a person named Sheila...)
无论是什么，我都已经替你清除了精神污染。现在，你最好<color=#dbc291ff>没事走两步</color>，感受一下有无其他不适症状。
Whatever it is, I have cleared the mental contamination for you. Now, you'd better <color=#dbc291ff>go for a walk</color>, and see if you have any other symptoms.
成功解决突发病例，我们继续吧——这次也是同样的情况吗？
Emergency resolved, now let's continue — same problem this time?
嗯，我最近好像总是在…忘记一些东西。
Well, I seem to keep... forgetting things lately.
之前我也告诉过你很多次，遗忘是人体非常正常的现象。
I have told you many times before that it is very normal to forget things sometimes.
不，我确信自己在忘记一些最基本的东西。比如，上一次生日是什么时候。我有哪些朋友。又或是，我是怎么来到空间站的。
No, I've been forgetting basic things. Like, my birthday, who my friends are. Or how I came to the space station.
这么说吧，为了让你彻底放心，这次我利用胶囊治疗舱，替你做了一份全身扫描，你的身体机能并无不妥。
Fine. Just to reassure you, I will give you a full-body scan using the capsule treatment module this time. There's nothing wrong with your physical functions.
相反，甚至有些…过于完美了。当然，这是你的福分，毕竟天赋异禀不是小概率事件，你的天赋分配，可能都点在了健康上。记忆力差了点，也在情理之中。

On the contrary, they're a little... too perfect. Of course, that's your gift. After all, having a talent is not an unusual occurrence, and your talent seems to be your physical health. So, in turn, it's only fair that you might have a poor memory.
这么完美的身体机能，有必要封存留档。我记录一下吧，你叫什么名字？
With such a perfect physical condition, I better keep this on file. Let me make a record of this. What's your name?
噢，好的，乔安医生。如果你需要知道的话，我叫……
Oh, yes, Dr. Joanne. My name is...
好了，看这位「新来的」脚下轻便，就算怪物突袭，也能立马灵活走位的样子。
Well, look at you, newcomer. You're so agile. You can easily escape from any monster raid.
（希拉•诺娃…那正是录像带中提到的名字。）
(Sheila Nova... That's the name mentioned in the video.)
（在影像记录的现实中，她明明离开了空间站。甚至…已经罹难了。）
(In the video recording, she clearly left the space station. She even... died.)
（这其中一定另有隐情，跟以斯帖提过的秘闻也对上了。）
(There must be something to this. It also matches the secret Esther was talking about.)
（还是优先调查一下这位「希拉」吧。）
(I should investigate this so-called Sheila first.)
别到处晃悠。你着了那些录像带的道，短时间内的意识紊乱够你喝一壶的。这就是好奇心的代价。
Don't wander around. You are affected by those videotapes, the consciousness disorder you're experiencing is no joke. This is the price you pay for being nosy.
有什么办法能让身体好受点吗？
Is there any way to alleviate the symptoms?
承蒙「丰饶」的慈怀，医疗舱散落着一些包治百病的药剂，你可以用用看。
Thanks to the kindness of Abundance, there are panaceas that can cure all diseases scattered around the medical cabin. You can try using them.
效果我虽不敢保证，但至少…比安慰剂强上那么一点儿。
I can't guarantee anything, but at least... They're a little better than placebos.
不，乔安科员，那绝对不是普通的自然遗忘，而是好像一夜之间，有人偷走了我的记忆……
No, Joanne, it's definitely not ordinary forgetfulness. It's like someone stole my memories overnight...
Maybe you had a run-in with a Memosnatcher.
你想说的是流光忆庭的激进派吧？因过分垂涎他人记忆，以致无所不用其极。如果真是他们所为，寻常的治疗确实无能为力。

Are you talking about those radicals in the Garden of Recollection? There's nothing those guys wouldn't do to get hold of other people's memories. If this is their doing, ordinary treatment is not going to work.
但据我所知，空间站中从未出现过窃忆者的行踪。
But as far as I know, those Memosnatchers have never been seen in the space station.
能偷走尴尬的回忆是好事啊！
It's good to have your embarrassing memories stolen!
这位{M#先生}{F#小姐}说话真是风趣。不过，我很确信我所遗忘的是非常重要的东西，甚至事关「我是谁」这样的命题……
That's a good one, {M#sir}{F#ma'am}. But I am sure what I have forgotten is very important, even questions like "who am I"...
依我看，只要不会影响正常的研究工作，那就不打紧。
The way I see it, it won't matter as long as it doesn't affect your research.
我有一些问题可以帮助你回忆。
I have some questions that may help you remember.
哦？没想到我们还遇上了野生的记忆大师。我倒要看看，你有什么连医疗科员都不知道的治疗方法。
Oh? We have a memory expert here, do we? I'd like to see what treatment you can come up with that even the medical staff don't know.
Are you the founder of the Dept. of Galactic Geopolitics?
不，{M#先生}{F#小姐}。我只是法政科的一介向导而已，日常负责传递空间站内大人物们所制定的规则。{M#先生}{F#小姐}如此发问，真是令我受宠若惊。

No, {M#sir}{F#ma'am}. I'm just a guide in the Department of Galactic Geopolitics. I'm responsible for passing on the rules made by the big shots in the space station. I'm flattered by your question though.
Have you ever had a lover or a husband?
不，{M#先生}{F#小姐}。我志愿将自己的一切都贡献给法政科，贡献给正义，包括我的健康、我的梦想和我最炙烈的感情。
No, {M#sir}{F#ma'am}. I hope to contribute my everything to the Department of Galactic Geopolitics, including my health, my dreams, and my passion.
Have you ever left the space station?
不，{M#先生}{F#小姐}。从我…降生以来，我从未离开过空间站。我想，在我魂归…银河之前，我也不会离开空间站。
No, {M#sir}{F#ma'am}. I've never left the space station since I was born. I don't think I'll leave the space station until my soul returns to the cosmos.
这些问题的答案与录像带不符…
The answers to these questions don't match the videos at all.
记忆大师，你不是在进行大记忆恢复术吗？怎么跟查户口似的，还扯上了录像带。
So, memory expert, aren't you gonna cast your memory restoration spell? You sound like you're doing a survey. And what's with the videos?
那些录像带里有希拉的过去。
Those videos are records of Sheila's past.
据我所知，录像带都如实记录了某位所有者的亲身经历。你的意思是说，你看到的录像带，正是希拉科员的人生备份？
As far as I know, all those videos are records of someone's personal experience. You mean those videos you saw are backups of Researcher Sheila's life?
可以再详细说说你都看到了些什么吗？
Can you elaborate on what you saw?
没什么，就当我自言自语吧。
Nothing. I'm just talking to myself.
我的好奇心倒被你勾起来了…你到底在录像带里看到了什么？
Now you've piqued my curiosity... What exactly did you see in the videos?
讲述在录像带中所见的内容……
You described what was shown in the videos.
太可怕了。这些事情我全都不记得，简直就像在倾听他人的人生。
That's horrible. I don't remember any of these things. It's like listening to someone else's life.
我替你们简单梳理一下。<color=#dbc291ff>在录像带里，希拉是法政科的创始人，曾经离开空间站，并且在返回母星预警「军团」入侵的路上罹难</color>——和眼前的这个人完全对不上嘛。

I'll try to sort it out for you guys. So, <color=#dbc291ff>in the videos, Sheila is the founder of the Department of Galactic Geopolitics, and she left the space station and died on the way back to her home planet to warn its inhabitants about the invasion of the Legion</color> — which doesn't match this person in front of us at all.
有没有可能是同名同姓的乌龙？
Maybe it's someone with the same name?
法政科登记了所有在编科员的名录，我很确信不会有人跟我持有同样的名字。
The Department of Galactic Geopolitics has registered the names of all its staff. I'm sure there's no one with the same name as me in the database.
怎么在这种事上你的记性倒是好得很啊！
How come you remember this so well?
实在是太古怪了。而且，抛开遗忘带来的强烈不安，我的脑海中一直记得一个画面，前所未有的清晰……
It's very strange. There is also an image that I remember very clearly above all else.
那是在一个昏暗的舱室里，伫立着<color=#dbc291ff>成排的监视屏</color>。
<color=#dbc291ff>Rows of security monitors</color> loom within a dark room.
无头无尾，无始无终。好像有人把它塞进了我的脑海，又像是一段完整记忆的残片。
The scene goes on forever without a beginning or an end. It is as though someone forced this image into my mind, but at the same time, it feels like a complete memory.
这位{M#先生}{F#小姐}，既然命运把你带到了这里与我相遇，我有一个请求，请你帮我前往这个似是而非的记忆地点，找到那些监视屏。
{M#Sir}{F#Ma'am}, since fate has brought us together, I have a request. Please help me get to the place in my memories and find those security monitors.
我有预感，如果能找到那些监视屏，一切疑虑都会解开。
I have a hunch that if I can just find those security monitors, everything will become clear.
Sure, I'll help you find it.
你们现在一唱一和，可别忘了，你们可都是病人。一个自述有短期失忆现象，一个陷入了短暂的意识紊乱。
Hey, don't forget, you are both patients. One has potential short-term amnesia, and the other had a temporary disturbance of consciousness.
如果根本找不到这样的地方呢？如果它彻头彻尾就只是你的一个幻想？
What if you can't find this place at all? What if it's just your imagination?
如果根本找不到这样的房间，那就把这一切当作是我的神经错乱和胡言乱语吧。
If you can't find this room at all, then maybe I am losing my mind.
屏幕上什么都没有，看来这并不是希拉口中的「监视屏」。
There is nothing on the screen. This doesn't seem to be the security monitor Sheila was talking about.
屏幕上的文本似乎和希拉有关，这应该就是她口中的「监视屏」。
The text on the screen seems to be talking about Sheila. Looks like this is the security monitor she speaks of.
希拉的记忆没有出错。这里的陈设，和她口述的记忆图景一模一样。
Sheila's memory is correct. This place is exactly how she described it.
也许她曾经来过这个地方。接下来要找的，就是她口中的监视屏。
Maybe she's been here before. Now, let's look for this security monitor that she was talking about.
首先，要破解这些由裂界晶簇构成的迷阵。
First, we need to get through this puzzle made of Fragmentum Crystals.
这《剧本》里的大部分内容，竟然与希拉回答问题所说的话一模一样。
This script is exactly what Sheila said when she answered my questions.
是有人在监视她，拓写她的日常言论？抑或是有人在操纵她，以她的口吻说出设定好的语言？
Is someone watching her and writing her dialogues? Or is someone controlling her speech?
还有这位古恩•科诺列夫，他所提到的实验到底是什么？
And this Gunn Konolev — what experiment was he talking about?
必须尽快把这些资料交还给希拉，再进一步分析这其中究竟有何隐情。
I have to get this information to Sheila as soon as possible. Then we can work out what the secret is.
这附近的晶簇是什么回事？
What's wrong with the crystals around here?
啊，你来迟了，这里没你什么事了。
Oh, you're too late. Everything's been dealt with.
What are you doing out here?
刚刚法政科的古恩过来暂时接管了医疗舱的权限，说他知道该怎么治好希拉。
Gunn from the Dept. of Galactic Geopolitics came and temporarily took over the authority of the medical cabin, saying he knows how to cure Sheila.
作为出借医疗舱的回报，他帮我们科室整理了医患隐私条例的细则，有了它，就算来访者有再大的本领，也要遵守科室的规定，无疑解决了我们平日的痛点，是对医患隐私的有效保障！

In return for letting him use the medical cabin, he helped our department compile a list of patient confidentiality clauses. With that, no matter how influential a visitor is, they must follow the department's rules. This will greatly improve the privacy of doctors and patients alike!
你也听到了，我们正在闭门制定医患隐私条例。
You heard her. We are working internally on a patient confidentiality regulation.
我连轴转好多天了，既然能偷个懒，何乐而不为？再说，希拉的身体本来也没问题。
I've been busy for days. Can't I take a little break? Besides, there's nothing wrong with Sheila's health.
This Gunn, he could be a bad guy...
这话从何说起？不过看你这么着急，也不像是在说谎，有什么话你们当面对质吧。
Why do you say that? Although, judging by your anxious look, you don't seem to be lying. Fine, you can sort it out with him face to face.
看你很急的样子…算了，反正和我们科室无关。你们的事，你们自己解决。
You look like you're in a rush... Whatever. Our department isn't involved. You can deal with your own problems.
希拉…重新上线。我们有些话要问你。
Sheila... reconnect. We have something to ask you.
…好的，古恩先生。但我现在有些不舒服。
Yes, Mr. Gunn... But I don't feel so good.
现在，你们可以对她发布几条指示，例如「祈祷」、「歌唱」、「呼喊」。
Now, you can give her some instructions, like pray, sing or shout.
务必先按照我说的做，记住，指令包括「祈祷」、「歌唱」、「呼喊」。
Be sure to do what I say first. Remember, the instructions include pray, sing and shout.
你好，我的名字是希拉•诺娃，「黑塔•银河法政科」的向导。
Hello, I am Sheila Nova, your guide to the Herta Department of Galactic Geopolitics.
Hey, hey, hey! What are you doing...
…虽然指令包括但不限于我刚才提到的三种，但显然这条不在《剧本》设置之中。
...Although the instructions are not limited to the three I just mentioned, clearly this is not in the script.
还是请你严格按照我说的做吧。
What the heck is going on?
没错，聪明人也许已经猜到了其中的秘密……
Yes, a smart person may have guessed by now...
乔安快步上前查看希拉的状况。
Joanne steps forward quickly to check on Sheila.
我正在帮法政科的年轻向导治疗。你们两位闯进来，是有什么事吗？
I'm performing treatment on this young guide from the Department of Galactic Geopolitics. Do you two have a good reason for breaking in here?
出了一点小意外，但是无伤大雅。
There was a little accident, but nothing serious.
她看起来只是短暂失去意识，生命体征并无大碍。
She seems to have temporarily lost consciousness, but her vitals are okay.
没错，乔安科员，我想我们之前已经取得了共识，希拉很快就会恢复正常，前提是阁下得在外面等着。
Yes, Researcher Joanne, I thought we have already reached this consensus before. Sheila will return to normal very soon, provided that you wait outside.
I think you have ulterior motives.
年轻人，我说过了，我正在帮法政科的年轻向导治疗。虽说出了一点小意外，但是无伤大雅，远远称不上另有阴谋。
Look, like I said, I'm helping this guide from the Department of Galactic Geopolitics. Although there was a little accident, it was not serious and far from being a conspiracy.
她看起来只是短暂失去意识，生命体征并无大碍。
She seems to have temporarily lost consciousness, but her vitals are okay.
I'm feeling a little sick, too...
不好意思，年轻人。如你所见，向导的治疗过程出了一点小意外，我不得不请阁下先出去，在外等上一阵。
Sorry, young one. As you can see, there was a little accident during the treatment procedure. I have to ask you to leave and wait outside for a while.
嗯，你哪里不舒服？找个阴凉地方我帮你看看！
Okay, what are your symptoms? Let's find a place to rest and I'll take a look at you!
对了，还未自我介绍吧。我是法政科的创始人古恩，如你们所见，我拥有与年龄相匹配的知识和权限。
By the way, I haven't introduced myself yet. I'm Gunn, the founder of the Department of Galactic Geopolitics. As you can see, I have the knowledge and authority that matches my age.
如果你们再不出去，我就要以干扰正常诊疗程序为由，传唤防卫科科员，把这位年轻人带走。
If you don't leave, I will have security take you away on the grounds of interfering with medical procedures.
没办法…他的权限比我要高，仅次于阿兰科长等人，咱们还是先去外面等着吧。
There's nothing we can do... His authority is higher than mine, second only to the likes of Chief Arlan. Let's wait outside for now.
不如先看看我手上的东西。
Why don't you take a look at what I have here?
你手上怎么拿着鄙人的手稿？在我印象中，它们已经尘封很久了。
Why do you have my manuscript? I distinctly remember putting it away a long time ago.
看你的样子，似乎对上面的内容有所误解。
It seems that you may have misunderstood its content.
正好让防卫科介入审讯。
Let's just call the Security Dept. on him now.
年轻人，你何出此言？别着急，我们之间也许有着什么误会。
Young {M#man}{F#lady}, why would you say that? Calm down, there seems to be some misunderstanding between us.
你手上怎么拿着鄙人的手稿？在我印象中，它们已经尘封很久了。
Why do you have my manuscript? I distinctly remember putting it away a long time ago.
不瞒你说，我唯一的爱好就是研究愚人戏，有时也会手痒难耐写些剧本。纯属虚构，当不得真。
To tell you the truth, I'm a theater enthusiast. I've written a play or two myself in my spare time. It's pure fiction.
既然你有很多疑惑，我们不妨心平气和地聊一聊，也许就能化干戈为玉帛。
I see you have a lot of doubts about me. Why don't we have a chat. Maybe we can be friends?
没错，你有什么要紧话，全都倒出来吧。
That's right. If you have anything important to say, spill it.
他的手稿提到了一种神秘实验。
This manuscript talks about a mysterious experiment.
所谓实验，无非就是我的个人兴趣，比如莳弄异星花草，摹写愚人戏的折子。
The so-called experiment is just a part of my hobby. Some people like gardening, I prefer to write plays.
你不会异想天开，怀疑跟你眼前所看到的景象有关吧？
Surely you can't be so naive as to think this has anything to do with what you see here right now, are you?
希拉在重复他事先写好的剧本。
Sheila has been reciting the script you wrote in advance.
…哦，你已经和她说过话了。真是个有心人，如果不仔细核对的话，是不会发现这一点的。
...Oh, you've spoken to her. That's very observant of you. Most people wouldn't notice this without paying close attention.
我这么说吧，所谓剧本只是我参考希拉的口吻，经过二次加工虚构创作而成。
Let me put it this way. I wrote that so-called script by imitating Sheila's speech. It is still fictional.
这个说法，应该能解释你的疑惑吧。
That should clear up any misunderstandings you may have.
所以我怀疑他没安好心。
I think you have ulterior motives.
这只是你的怀疑而已，你还有什么其他的证据吗？如果没有，不必再继续聊了。
That's just your personal speculation. Do you have any other evidence? If not, there's no need to continue this conversation.
My intuition has always been accurate.
I am not going to leave.
乔安你别光在旁边看着。
你在胡言乱语些什么呀…不会是随便偷看录像带留下的后遗症吧？
What are you talking about... Is this another residual effect of watching those videos?
…什么录像带？我不明白你在说什么。
...What videos? What are you talking about?
I saw you on the videos.
不对呀，你怎么可能在希拉的录像带里见过古恩呢？
What? How could you have seen Gunn in Sheila's videos?
按照如今两人的年龄差推算，如果你在录像带里见到了年轻的古恩，那么彼时的希拉一定还只是个襁褓中的婴儿——这和录像带对不上。
Given their age difference, if you saw a young Gunn in the videos, Sheila must've been a baby at the time — but that's not what you saw.
除非，面前的希拉，根本就不是录像带的主人，也不是真正的希拉•诺娃。
Unless, the Sheila in front of us now is neither the owner of the videos nor the real Sheila Nova.
如果有所谓阴谋，所谓实验，那只能说明根本没有什么同名同姓的乌龙，而是她——冒用了希拉的名字。
If there is a so-called conspiracy or some kind of experiment, then it can only mean one thing. She is impersonating Sheila under false pretenses.
并且严格按照古恩的剧本生活。
And she is strictly following Gunn's script.
嗯，补上这句显得更有气势了。
Yep, it all makes sense now.
我认为乔安的推理完全正确。
I think Joanne's theory is correct.
除非实验是让希拉青春永驻…
Unless the experiment is to keep Sheila young forever...
我听说，全银河唯有「丰饶」的使者可以令人形寿无疆，芳龄永驻…古恩先生应该没这个实力。
It is said that, in the entire cosmos, only the messenger of Abundance has the power to make people live forever... Surely, Mr. Gunn doesn't have this ability.
呵呵，你们两个一唱一和，真是好一段精彩的推理。
Hehe, you two... I must say, this is all very entertaining to watch.
也罢，你竟然还找到了录像带…看来是瞒不住了。没想到这么一个小小的实验，也会引来外人的兴趣。
Well, since you've found the video... I suppose I can't hide it anymore. Who would've thought this tiny experiment of mine could attract so much interest.
接下来要发生的事情，可能会使你们大跌眼镜，但无论你们看到的或是听到的有多么耸人听闻，也请耐心等到最后。
What happens next may surprise you, but no matter what you see or hear, please be patient until the end.
心里竟然有些隐隐的期待…
最好如实交代，不要添油加醋。
You better tell the truth. Don't embellish it.
你想多了，我什么世面没见过？
Get over yourself. There's nothing I haven't seen.
走到今天这个局面，都是因为我最初的实验设计太过理想——以为依靠剧本和技术，就能还原出一个栩栩如生的希拉。
This all happened because I was too idealistic with my initial experiment design — I thought I could restore Sheila to her original self relying only on a script and some technology.
可惜依靠有限的剧本，终究还是不能模拟人在多数情况下的正常反应。
Unfortunately, a limited script will never be able to simulate a person's natural reaction in the real world.
所以我试图让她接触过去的录像带，获得更多人的认知。
So I tried letting her watch videos from the past, hoping that she could get a better understanding of how to be human.
但我没想到，这个希拉——又或者说，原型机<unbreak>157</unbreak>的认知能力已经超出了我的想象。
But I didn't expect Sheila, or should I say, prototype <unbreak>157</unbreak>, to become so human.
她越来越像一个真正的人那样思考，意识到这世上曾有过一模一样的希拉，以至于开始怀疑自己的存在。

She started to think more and more like a real person. She even realized that another Sheila, who looked exactly like her, once existed in this world. That was when she began to doubt her own existence.
这对一个仿生人来说，是非常危险的。
This is very dangerous for an android.
难怪之前对希拉进行身体扫描，发现她的各项参数近乎完美——简直就像是精密设定的产物。
No wonder her body scan showed that her parameters were almost perfect — like a well-calibrated machine.
顾名思义，就是极度形似真人的机器人——你好好听故事别打岔……
As the name suggests, it is a robot that looks extremely similar to a real person. Now pay attention to Gunn's story and don't interrupt.
老实说，我也大开眼界。难怪之前对希拉进行身体扫描，发现她的各项参数近乎完美——简直就像是精密设定的产物。
To be honest, it was an eye-opener for me as well. No wonder her body scan showed that her parameters were almost perfect — it was like a well-calibrated machine.
你不要一副正经评论的样子啊！
Stop talking like an art critic!
难怪之前对希拉进行身体扫描，发现她的各项参数近乎完美——简直就像是精密设定的产物。
No wonder why her body scan showed a perfect reading — like a well-calibrated machine.
没错，我所谓的实验，正是你们面前的这个与真人无异的年轻希拉。
That's right. The experiment I referred to is this perfect recreation of a young Sheila standing before you.
而她的原型，法政科的联合创始人，以及…我的爱人，希拉•诺娃，早在很久以前就已经葬身于茫茫星海之中。
She was modeled after the co-founder of the Department of Galactic Geopolitics, and... the love of my life, Sheila Nova. She died in the vast ocean of stars a long time ago.
我之所以制作原型机<unbreak>157</unbreak>，只是为了弥补当初没有救下爱人的遗憾。
The reason I made prototype <unbreak>157</unbreak> is that I wanted to make up for my regret of not saving my true love when I had the chance.
当这个替身知晓自己并非真人之后，为了防止她产生认知错乱，我不得不再三地抹去她的记忆，还原设置，维持她的正常运转。
When she found out that she is not a real person, in order to prevent cognitive confusion, I had to erase her memory again and again, to restore her settings and maintain her normal operation.
所以希拉才患上了短期失忆症。
That's why Sheila had short-term amnesia.
嗯…但我没想到，她会不断主动向外界求援，直到这一切被你们发现。
Right... But I never expected she would keep asking for help from the outside world until you eventually found out about all this.
可她还记得你藏匿资料的地方。
But she still remembered where you hid your information.
是我小看了她，或者说，她已经超出了我的实验想象。
I underestimated her, or rather, she has surpassed my imagination.
你不该剥夺她自主思考的能力。
You shouldn't deprive her of the ability to think for herself.
不，恰恰我认为这种剥夺是必要的。
No, I actually think this deprivation is necessary.
她对失忆现象的察觉、向你们求援的举动，都暗藏着实验失控的风险。
When she became aware of her own memory loss and began to ask around for help, the situation could've easily gone out of control.
所以，当听说希拉正在医疗舱接受乔安科员的治疗，我就第一时间赶来，试图再次「修正」她的感知。
So when I found out that Sheila was being treated by Researcher Joanne in the medical cabin, I came as soon as I could to try to "fix" her cognition once again.
视概率而定。也许一劳永逸，她再度按照剧本生活。也许不尽如人意，她依然陷入失忆、求助、修正的循环。

There are many possibilities. Maybe she will finally live according to the script once and for all. Or perhaps she will continue the cycle, fall into another amnesia, and begin to seek help, until the next fix.
事已至此，我的确无法再一意孤行。
Indeed, I cannot let this cycle go on any longer.
不论我们如何处理希拉，我都拜托你们，不要将这个实验公之于众。这其中涉及的伦理问题，必然会受到法政科的追究。
No matter how we deal with Sheila, I beg you, please don't make this experiment public. The ethical issues are bound to be investigated by the Department of Galactic Geopolitics.
就当是保全我这个老人悼念亡妻的小小祈愿吧。
Please, forgive this old man who was just trying to mourn his dead wife.
我只是个旁观者，我说了不算。{NICKNAME}，我想现在有三条路摆在你的面前。
I'm just a bystander, it's not my decision. {NICKNAME}, I think you have three choices right now.
一，告知原型机<unbreak>157</unbreak>她的身世真相。
1. Tell Prototype <unbreak>157</unbreak> the truth about her life.
二，修正并还原原型机<unbreak>157</unbreak>的出厂设置。
三，销毁原型机<unbreak>157</unbreak>，将这个危险的实验从空间站的历史中抹去。
3. Destroy Prototype <unbreak>157</unbreak> and erase all records of this dangerous experiment from the history of the space station.
作为钓出这一切隐秘的主导者，就由你来作出最后的决定吧——至于古恩，就由我来看着他。
As the leader of this investigation, it's up to you to make the final decision. As for Gunn, I'll keep an eye on him.
Have you really thought it through?
好吧，既然你们都这么坚持，那我会告知原型机<unbreak>157</unbreak>有关她身世的来龙去脉。
Well, since you insist, I'll tell Prototype <unbreak>157</unbreak> about her story.
至于她会做何反应，就不是我能控制的了。
But how she is gonna react to that is completely beyond my control.
谢谢你，年轻人，我认为你做出了正确的选择。
Thank you, young one. I think you made the right choice.
我会修正并还原原型机<unbreak>157</unbreak>的出厂设置，今后，她将继续履行法政科向导的职责，与往常无异。
I will fix and factory reset Prototype <unbreak>157</unbreak>. And then, she will continue to perform her duties as a guide for the Department of Galactic Geopolitics, as usual.
虽然这个选择违背了我悼念亡妻的初衷，但是考虑到原型机<unbreak>157</unbreak>失控的风险，我不得不承认这个选择颇富远见。

This would go against my original wish of mourning my deceased wife, but considering the risks that Prototype <unbreak>157</unbreak> poses should she become out of control, I must admit that this is a safe choice.
我答应你，从这里离开后，我会趁无人之时到嘘实埋间去，销毁原型机<unbreak>157</unbreak>。
I promise you this. When I leave, I will choose a quiet time to bring Prototype <unbreak>157</unbreak> with me to the disposal site and destroy her there.
放心吧，不会有任何人发现。毕竟那里是除了议事间之外我最熟悉的地方，长眠着无数我呕心沥血却终告失败的实验品。
Don't worry, no one will find out. After all, it is the place I am most familiar with outside of the Conference Room. My countless previous failed experimental objects sleep there.
古恩执行了你的决策，并沉默地离开了医疗舱。
Gunn carries out your decision and leaves the medical cabin in silence.
我好像做了一场非常冗长的梦。
Like I just had a very long dream.
我梦见了冰冷的空间站、诀别的爱人、巨大的星球、以及沉默的无线电。
I dreamed of a cold space station, a lost lover, a huge planet, and a silent radio.
仿生人会梦见电子羊吗…
Can an android dream of electric sheep?
哈哈，不，{M#先生}{F#小姐}，她不会。相反，她梦见了冰冷的空间站、诀别的爱人、巨大的星球、以及沉默的无线电。
Haha, no, {M#sir}{F#ma'am}. Actually, she dreamed of a cold space station, a lost lover, a huge planet, and a silent radio.
所以，我是一个活生生的人…的机械替身。我的人生是对另一个逝者的拙劣模仿。
So, I'm a mechanical replica... of a real person. My life is a parody of the dead.
我的思想、话语乃至日复一日的行为，都是古恩先生事先设定好的产物。
My thoughts, my speech, and even my behavior are the product of Mr. Gunn's preset.
在得知了「另一个我」曾有过那样丰富的人生，我多么希望，我也有属于自己的生日，属于自己的朋友，以及属于自己的梦想。
After learning that the "other me" had such a rich life, I wish I had my own birthday, my own friends and my own dreams.
Take your time. Everything will happen in due course.
是<color=#eb4d3dff>我多么希望</color>吗，也许<color=#eb4d3dff>我也有属于自己的生日，属于自己的朋友，以及属于自己的梦想</color>吧。
Is it <color=#eb4d3dff>my wish?</color> Or perhaps <color=#eb4d3dff>I do have my own birthday, my own friends, and my own dream</color>.
你可以继承希拉的一切。
You can inherit everything from Sheila.
是<color=#eb4d3dff>我多么希望</color>吗，也许<color=#eb4d3dff>我也有属于自己的生日，属于自己的朋友，以及属于自己的梦想</color>吧。
Is it <color=#eb4d3dff>my wish?</color> Or perhaps <color=#eb4d3dff>I do have my own birthday, my own friends, and my own dream</color>.
不好！她的语言模块似乎出现了问题，各项机能指标正在急剧波动。
Oh no! There seems to be a problem with her language module. Her various functional indicators are fluctuating sharply.
谢谢你<color=#eb4d3dff>我只是法政科的一介向导而已</color>{NICKNAME}。我想还有一场<color=#eb4d3dff>我志愿将自己的一切都贡献给法政科</color>更长的梦在等着我。

Thank you, {NICKNAME}. <color=#eb4d3dff>I'm just a guide in the Department of Galactic Geopolitics</color>. I think there is an even longer dream waiting for me. One where <color=#eb4d3dff>I willingly dedicate everything to the department</color>.
她的认知模块因为信息过载被烧毁了…看来将所有真相都告知她，是一个错误的选择。
Her cognitive module was burned due to information overload... It seems that telling her the truth was a bad decision.
可惜浪费了如此精细的实验品！就算是医学诊断也无法察觉她与真人的不同。除了…在她的脖颈侧面，隐隐约约写着<unbreak>157</unbreak>的编号。

Alas! Even the most advanced diagnostic equipment couldn't detect the difference between her and a real person. Except... on the side of her neck, there is a vague mark with the number <unbreak>157</unbreak>.
——别太自责，谁也不会料到这样的结局，这不是你的错。
No need to blame yourself. No one could have foreseen this outcome. It's not your fault.
对了，你那里还存着希拉的录像带吧？是时候把它们归还到应去的地方。
By the way, you still have Sheila's videos there? It's time to return them to their rightful place.
打起精神来，就让一切回到原点吧。
Cheer up and let everything go back to the way it was.
我感觉…好极了。你好，乔安科员，好久不见。
I feel... great. Hello, Researcher Joanne. Long time no see.
Actually, you only just met not long ago.
哦是吗？可我好像什么都记不得了。
Oh, really? I don't seem to remember anything.
这位{M#先生}{F#小姐}，我们是第一次见吧。我的名字是希拉•诺娃，如果你需要知道的话。
{M#Sir}{F#Ma'am}, this is the first time we've met. My name is Sheila Nova, if you want to know that.
珍惜这种好极了的感觉吧。
Please cherish how great you are feeling right now.
这位{M#先生}{F#小姐}，我们是第一次见吧。我的名字是希拉•诺娃，如果你需要知道的话。
{M#Sir}{F#Ma'am}, this is the first time we've met. My name is Sheila Nova, if you want to know that.
又开始了…无尽的循环。不过她的身体指数一切正常，对她来说，一个中庸的收场不失为一个最好的结局。
Again... Endless cycle. However, her health parameters appear normal. For her, this ending may be the best outcome.
这么精细的实验品，只要存在，就是一种艺术。毕竟连医学诊断也无法察觉她与真人的不同。除了…在她的脖颈侧面，隐藏着一个<unbreak>157</unbreak>的编号。

Such a fine piece of technological experimentation, its mere existence is a work of art. Even the most advanced diagnostic equipment couldn't detect the difference between her and a real person. Except... on the side of her neck, there is a vague mark with the number <unbreak>157</unbreak>.
对了，你那里还存着希拉的录像带吧？是时候把它们归还到应去的地方。
